name: Chicken Tikka Masala

First short marinade: 700 g chicken breast cut in thin cubes (or chicken thighs, boneless)
Second marinade: 0.5-1 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder (don't substitute it entirely with paprika, the flavor is completely different)
1 tsp ginger garlic paste (substitute with half ginger half garlic)
Sauce: 4 crushed tomatoes (or 300 g passata, assuming 1 kg yields 600 g of passata. Tomatoes are better, to differentiate it from Chicken Makhani that needs to be smooth)
Green chili (1, deseeded, slit or chopped)
Cashews (28 of them. Roasted cashews are also fine.)
Finishing: 0.5 tbsp Kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves, crushed)

instructions: - Prepare the chicken in medium cubes, removing bones and extra fat.
- Add some salt to it, leave aside.
- Spread it on the chicken and leave it covered for at least 8 h.
- Leave the cashews in a bit of boiling water, to soften up.
You can either do this in a pan or in the oven. I use the oven. After pre-heating, set at 220ºC (convection) for 21-22'.
- On a small pan cook the onion with 2 tbsp of oil and the salt, on medium heat and regular movement
- While it's in the oven, in a (preferably high) pot, cook the crushed tomato with the other 1 tbsp of oil. When it dries it will start caramelizing, which is nice to counteract its own acidity.
- Meanwhile prepare the sauce spice mix and finely blend the cashews. Add the spice when the tomato is dry.
- Keep stirring around. It will be ready when it clumps up together, not sticking to the pot.

- Take the chicken out of the oven and, in the same oven pot, combine everything: the paste, chicken and cashew cream, with about 400 mL of water (but measuring how much water you need is trial and error).
- After simmering for some time, you should be able to see traces of fat on top. Add heavy cream and reduce for 2-3'.
- Turn off the heat and serve. Optionally add the fenugreek and coriander to each plate.

culinary: Be strict with the oven time and avoid burning the tomato/spices.
Some people add onions. But the whole point of butter chicken is having a silky smooth sauce. Onions will mess with it.
If you are not used to hot spicy food, substitute some of the Kashmiri chili powder with sweet paprika.
